msg.stdout:
# create-mill

[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/create-mill.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/create-mill)

A generator for self-building repos. Answer some questions, push to GitHub, and let the agent build your project.

## How it works

```
npx create-mill my-project
```

1. Scaffolds a repo with a `PROJECT.md` (your spec) and GitHub Actions workflow
2. You push to GitHub and add your `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret
3. A cron runs every 5 minutes - Claude reads your spec and builds the project
4. Watch it go, or open issues to nudge it

## The loop

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  cron (every 5 min)                             │
│    ↓                                            │
│  another run in progress? → exit                │
│    ↓                                            │
│  .mill/initialized exists?                      │
│    no  → bootstrap prompt (create structure)    │
│    yes → iterate prompt (improve/extend)        │
│    ↓                                            │
│  claude runs, commits to main                   │
│    ↓                                            │
│  agent decides it's done? → disables workflow   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Stopping the mill

- Use the `mill-control` workflow to disable/enable
- Or: let the agent decide it's complete (it will disable the workflow)

## Status

Early development. See [TODO.md](./TODO.md).

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies (none currently)
npm install

# Link locally to test
npm link

# Test the CLI
create-mill test-project

# Unlink when done
npm unlink -g create-mill

# Publish to npm
npm publish
```

content.text:
Here’s a quick summary of the README:

- **`create-mill`** is an npm generator for creating “self-building” repos.
- You run `npx create-mill my-project`, answer prompts, then push the generated repo to GitHub.
- The repo includes a `PROJECT.md` spec and a GitHub Actions workflow.
- After adding `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` as a GitHub secret, a cron job runs every 5 minutes:
  - checks if another run is active,
  - bootstraps on first run or iterates on existing project,
  - runs Claude, commits changes to `main`,
  - and can disable itself when it decides the project is done.
- You can stop/start automation via a **`mill-control` workflow**, or let the agent stop automatically.
- Project is in **early development** (see `TODO.md`).
- README also includes local dev/publish commands (`npm link`, `create-mill test-project`, `npm publish`).

- The repo includes a `PROJECT.md` spec and a GitHub Actions workflow.
- After adding `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` as a GitHub secret, a cron job runs every 5 minutes:
  - checks if another run is active,
  - bootstraps on first run or iterates on existing project,
  - runs Claude, commits changes to `main`,
  - and can disable itself when it decides the project is done.
- You can stop/start automation via a **`mill-control` workflow**, or let the agent stop automatically.
- Project is in **early development** (see `TODO.md`).
- README also includes local dev/publish commands (`npm link`, `create-mill test-project`, `npm publish`).
